尽管可能感觉竞选活动一直在进行，而且将永远持续下去，但线性时间无情地向前推进，我们终于距离2020年美国总统大选整整两个月了。由于新冠肺炎的大流行，仅后勤一项今年就比以往任何时候都复杂，全国各地的选民可能会遇到这样或那样的复杂情况。
现在，我们将Facebook加入到这个环境中。该公司在选举中被用作错误信息和操纵工具的记录不佳。在Facebook今天的一篇帖子中，公司首席执行官马克·扎克伯格概述了公司今年将采取的一系列新措施，以保护我们的民主。唉，其中一些措施感觉就像是在马匹很久以前离开时关上了谷仓门，以至于你甚至忘记了自己曾经有过马厩门。
这场选举不会像往常一样，扎克伯格准确地开始了。关于投票、选举和两位总统候选人的错误信息已经在Facebook和其他所有媒体平台上猖獗，而且正在由国内外演员传播。那么，Facebook将为此做些什么呢？扎克伯格承诺，Facebook将帮助人们注册和投票，澄清人们对这次选举将如何进行的困惑，并采取措施减少暴力和动乱的可能性。
几周来，Facebook一直在Facebook和Instagram上插入其选民信息中心。该登录页面使用用户的位置-如果启用，是近似的或具体的-来显示选民登记信息、邮寄投票资格、选民ID要求以及其他州和当地信息。它还包含一个与投票相关的事实核查部分，涵盖的主题包括，面对面投票和邮寄投票在美国都有悠久的可信性历史。
扎克伯格说，这种情况将继续下去，公司不仅会继续执行反对选民压制的政策，而且会扩大这些政策：
我们已经消除了关于如何或何时投票的明确虚假陈述，这些陈述可能会导致某人失去投票机会-例如，像这样的话，你可以在选举日之后3天内邮寄选票，这显然不是真的。(在大多数州，邮寄选票必须在选举日之前收到，而不仅仅是邮寄，才能被计算在内。)。我们现在正在扩大这项政策，将关于投票的隐性失实陈述也包括在内，比如我听说任何持有驾照的人今年都会获得选票，因为这可能会误导你，让你不知道需要做什么才能获得选票，即使这本身并不一定会使你的选票无效。
Facebook正在全面扩大其事实核查范围，在试图使选举结果合法化或讨论投票方法合法性的内容上添加标签，例如，声称合法的投票方法将导致欺诈。
不幸的是，这种错误信息的主要来源是现任美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)。Facebook在核实特朗普或他的竞选团队的说法方面一直进展缓慢，但它似乎确实在为明确涉及选民错误信息的帖子贴上标签。例如，这家社交媒体巨头将特朗普今天在美国东部时间上午10点45分左右分享的这篇帖子贴上了标签：
东海岸的投票从东部时间晚上7点开始关闭，并在接下来的几个小时里继续波澜壮阔地关闭。通常，数百万美国人盯着他们最喜欢的有线电视或广播网络等待，等待返回和每个州的电话。然而，正如我们在2000年了解到的那样，报道是按照自己的节奏进行的-而不是在迫切需要新闻的观众希望他们这样做的时候。
虽然加利福尼亚州或阿拉巴马州等州的结果可能已成定局，但由于预计邮寄和缺席投票的增加，我们可能同样需要等待几个小时甚至几天才能了解关键摇摆州的最终结果。Facebook现在正在努力教育其2亿多美国用户，这种等待并不是欺诈性的，相反，这是系统正常工作的迹象。
扎克伯格说：我们将利用投票信息中心让人们做好准备，以应对可能需要一段时间才能得到正式结果的情况。这些信息将帮助人们理解，在选举之夜没有结果并没有什么不正当之处。
Facebook还将在选举日当天及之后开展工作，对任何没有得到其合作伙伴路透社(Reuters)和国家选举池(National Options Pool)支持的胜利声明进行事实核查。选举模块将在结果出来时显示出来，Facebook将在结果公布时主动通知人们。扎克伯格补充说，最重要的是，如果任何候选人或竞选团队试图在结果公布前宣布胜利，我们会在他们的帖子上贴上标签，教育人们官方结果还没有公布，并引导人们看到官方结果。
扎克伯格说，该平台还试图加强对民兵、Qanon等阴谋网和其他可能在选举后组织暴力或内乱的组织的执法力度，扎克伯格说，这一平台还试图加强对民兵、Qanon等阴谋网络的执法，以及可能在选举后的一段时间内组织暴力或内乱的其他组织。
一年多前，美国联邦调查局(FBI)就认定包括QAnon在内的极端主义运动是威胁，但直到最近，这些内容才被允许在社交网络上扩散。Twitter在7月份禁止了Qanon活动，称其为线下传播到现实世界伤害的协同有害活动。这场阴谋运动与几起暴力事件有关，包括一起绑架和一起谋杀案。
然而，Facebook一直以来都没有对暴力威胁采取行动，直到为时已晚。大约两周前，该公司对Qanon进行了打击，删除了数千个Facebook和Instagram群组、页面和账户。
牛顿写道，从表面上看，[QAnon]似乎是一个笑话，就像TidePods偷偷尝起来很好吃一样。但就像洗衣粉一样，数以千计的美国人现在已经被QAnon毒死，其后果似乎更可怕、更持久。
note_en:
Although it may feel like the campaigns have been going on forever and will continue forever, linear time inexorably marches on and we are, at last, exactly two months away from the 2020 US presidential election. The  logistics alone are more complicated than ever this year, thanks to the COVID-19 pandemic, and voters around the nation are likely to encounter  complications of one kind or another.
 Into this milieu we now add Facebook. The company has a  bad track record of being used as a tool of misinformation and manipulation when it comes to elections. In  a Facebook post today, company CEO Mark Zuckerberg outlined a whole bunch of new steps the company will be taking to "protect our democracy" this year. Some of those measures, alas, feel like shutting the barn door when the horse left so long ago you forgot you ever even had one.
 "This election is not going to be business as usual," Zuckerberg began, accurately. Misinformation about voting, the election, and both candidates for the presidency is already rampant on Facebook and every other media platform, and it's being spread by actors both foreign and domestic. So what is Facebook going to do about it? "Helping people register and vote, clearing up confusion about how this election will work, and taking steps to reduce the chances of violence and unrest," Zuckerberg promised.
  Facebook has for several weeks been plugging its voter information center in both Facebook and Instagram. That landing page takes a user's location—approximate or specific, if enabled—to display voter-registration information, vote-by-mail eligibility, voter ID requirements, and other state and local information. It also contains a voting-related fact-checking section, covering topics such as, "Both voting in person and voting by mail have a long history of trustworthiness in the US."
 That will continue, Zuckerberg said, and the company will not only continue to enforce its policies against voter suppression, but expand them:
 We already remove explicit misrepresentations about how or when to vote that could cause someone to lose their opportunity to vote—for example, saying things like "you can send in your mail ballot up to 3 days after election day", which is obviously not true. (In most states, mail-in ballots have to be *received* by election day, not just mailed, in order to be counted.) We're now expanding this policy to include implicit misrepresentations about voting too, like "I hear anybody with a driver's license gets a ballot this year", because it might mislead you about what you need to do to get a ballot, even if that wouldn't necessarily invalidate your vote by itself.
 Facebook is expanding its fact-checking in general to add labels to content that "seeks to delegitimize the outcome of the election or discuss the legitimacy of voting methods, for example, by claiming that lawful methods of voting will lead to fraud."
 Unfortunately, a primary source of exactly that kind of misinformation is the sitting US president, Donald Trump. Facebook has been  slow to fact-check claims made by Trump or his campaign, but it does seem to be making good on appending labels to posts explicitly relating to voter misinformation. For example, the social media giant labeled this post Trump shared today around 10:45am EDT:
  The labels, however, require a user to click through to find out what, exactly, the facts might be.
  Zuckerberg also announced that Facebook will not accept any new "political or issue ads" during the final week of the campaign—so, beginning around October 27.
 "In the final days of an election there may not be enough time to contest new claims," he wrote, explaining the suspension. However: "Advertisers will be able to continue running ads they started running before the final week and adjust the targeting for those ads, but those ads will already be published transparently in our Ads Library so anyone, including fact-checkers and journalists, can scrutinize them."
 Unfortunately, the ads that political advertisers place before the deadline are allowed to be full of lies, and Facebook will not fact-check them. That's been the company's  long-standing policy, and it evidently has no intention of reversing course for the rest of this campaign season. Fact-checkers and journalists  will be scrutinizing the ads, as quickly as they can, but they may not have the same reach as a paid-for ad campaign that could be actively spreading falsehoods.
 The final week before November 3 will no doubt be a valuable homestretch for campaigns—but it may prove less important than in previous years, as voters nationwide are expected to take advantage of early voting, mail-in voting, and mailed or in-person absentee voting to avoid exposure to groups and crowding in the continuing pandemic.
 The Trump campaign vigorously objected to Facebook's decision. "In the last seven days of the most important election in our history, President Trump will be banned from defending himself on the largest platform in America," the campaign’s deputy national press secretary, Samantha Zager, said in a statement, adding that the president will be "silenced by the Silicon Valley Mafia."
 This is, of course, also false; Facebook's prohibition only applies to paid advertising, not posts created or shared by any individual, page, or campaign. Neither will it prevent Trump (or his rival, Democratic candidate Joe Biden) from issuing new ads on any other broadcast, print, or online platform or stop him from holding press conferences, delivering interviews, hiring a skywriter, or getting his message out in any and every other way.
  Facebook's stance with content by and large tends to be reactive, not proactive. The company is, however, proactively looking at ways to mitigate what are extremely likely to be high-tension feelings on election night itself.
 Polls begin closing on the East Coast at 7pm ET and continue to close in waves over the following several hours. Typically, millions of Americans wait with their eyes glued to their favorite cable or broadcast network waiting for returns to come in and each state to be called. As we  learned in 2000, however, reports come in at their own pace—not when viewers desperate for news want them to.
 While the outcome in a state such as California or Alabama is  likely to be a foregone conclusion, we are just as likely to have to wait hours or perhaps even days to learn the final outcomes in key swing states thanks to the projected increase in mail-in and absentee voting. Facebook is working now to try to teach its more than 200 million US users that this wait is not fraudulent and is, instead, a sign the system is working properly.
 "We'll use the Voting Information Center to prepare people for the possibility that it may take a while to get official results," Zuckerberg said. "This information will help people understand that there is nothing illegitimate about not having a result on election night."
 Facebook will also work on and after Election Day to fact-check any claim of victory that is not backed up by its partners, Reuters and the National Election Pool. The election module will show results as they come in, and Facebook will "notify people proactively" as those results become available. Most importantly, Zuckerberg added, "If any candidate or campaign tries to declare victory before the results are in, we'll add a label to their post educating that official results are not yet in and directing people to the official results."
 The platform is also trying to strengthen enforcement against "militias, conspiracy networks like QAnon, and other groups that could be used to organize violence or civil unrest in the period after the elections," Zuckerberg said.
 The FBI determined extremist movements, including QAnon, to be threats  more than a year ago, but the content was allowed to proliferate on social networks until very recently. Twitter banned QAnon activity  in July, calling it "coordinated harmful activity" that spread offline into real-world injury. The conspiracy movement has been linked to several violent episodes, including  a kidnapping and  a murder.
 Facebook, however, has a record of  failing to act against violent threats until it's too late. The company cracked down on QAnon about two weeks ago,  removing thousands of Facebook and Instagram groups, pages, and accounts.
  "On its face, [QAnon] seems no less a joke than the idea that TidePods secretly taste delicious," Newton wrote. "But as with the laundry detergent, thousands of Americans have now been poisoned by QAnon, and the consequences seem likely to be far more dire, and long-lasting."
title: Facebook防止选举错误信息的计划：允许它，主要是
title_en: Facebook’s plan to prevent election misinformation: Allowing it, mostly

note:
火箭实验室发出这一信号已经有一段时间了；光子卫星平台在去年年初宣布，并在3月份收购了航天器制造商辛克莱星际公司。这只是该公司何时选择按下按钮的问题，现在它已经这样做了。
正如贝克在今天的一次直播中所解释的那样(现在First Light已经成功地部署到轨道上)，该公司认为“进入太空”在许多方面都是一个解决的问题，尽管存在固有的风险。他说，下一个最大的痛点是“从一个想法到把东西送入轨道真的很痛苦。”
他说，当一个项目可以在18个月内从想法进入轨道时，这是值得庆祝的。这太慢了，跟不上当地的创新，特别是对可能没有18个月跑道的初创企业来说。“我们需要解决这个问题，”贝克说。
Photon和First Light代表了火箭实验室的新业务主张，即为现代卫星提供灵活的平台，并与其电子运载火箭和其他服务齐头并进。当然，在整个过程中充当合作伙伴，而不仅仅是发射提供商，对Rocket Lab来说当然是更多的工作和资金，如果事情进展顺利，对客户来说也可以更快、更便宜。
随着环月和行星际空间成为电子发射的目标，还将有其他新版本的光子。火箭实验室已经签约执行月球任务，这是美国国家航空航天局(NASA)的实验顶石飞船，它将以光子为基础，帮助为以后的阿耳特弥斯任务扫清道路。
note_en:
Rocket Lab has telegraphed this move for some time;  the Photon satellite platform was announced early last year, and in March it  acquired spacecraft maker Sinclair Interplanetary. It was just a matter of when the company would choose to press the button, and it has now done so.
 As Beck explained in a live broadcast today (now that First Light has successfully deployed into orbit), the company felt that “access to space” is, in many ways and despite the inherent risks, a solved problem. The next biggest pain point, he said, is that “it’s just really painful to go from an idea to getting something in orbit.”
  It’s cause for celebration, he said, when a project can go from idea to orbit in 18 months. That’s far too slow to keep up with innovation on the ground, especially for startups, which may not have 18 months of runway. “We need to fix that,” Beck said.
  Photon and First Light represent Rocket Lab’s new business proposition of providing a flexible platform for a modern satellite, and one that fits hand-in-glove with its Electron launch vehicle and other services. Acting as a partner throughout the process rather than just the launch provider is of course more work and money for Rocket Lab, and if things go well it could be much faster and cheaper for the customer as well.
 There will be other, new versions of Photon as well, as cislunar and interplanetary space become targets for Electron launches. Rocket Lab is already signed on for a lunar mission,  NASA’s experimental CAPSTONE craft, which will be based on Photon and help clear the way for later Artemis missions.
title: 火箭实验室秘密发射了它的第一颗卫星“第一光”
title_en: Rocket Lab secretly launched its very first satellite, ‘First Light’

note:
特朗普周三在接受当地电视台采访时发表了最初的评论。特朗普说：“他们将投票，然后他们将不得不通过去投票站以这种方式投票来检查自己的选票，因为如果投票结果列成表格，他们就无法做到这一点。”
“那就让他们寄进来，让他们去投票吧。如果制度真的像他们说的那么好，那么他们显然就不能投票了。“。
周四，Twitter在与这些评论相关的两条推文中添加了一条“公共利益通知”，理由是该公司关于公民和选举诚信的规定。Twitter发言人尼克·帕西里奥(Nick Pacilio)表示，这些推文违反了“专门鼓励人们从事可能破坏个人投票完整性的行为”的规定。Twitter已经限制了这些推文的覆盖范围，并限制了没有评论的点赞、回复和转发。
特朗普最近对邮寄投票的攻击也越界了Facebook。该公司将删除特朗普最近投票评论的任何视频，这些视频在没有上下文的情况下被分享，或者那些支持总统声明的视频，尽管到目前为止还没有确认任何视频。
“这段视频违反了我们禁止选民欺诈的政策，我们将删除它，除非它被分享以纠正记录，”Facebook政策沟通总监安迪·斯通(Andy Stone)说。
Facebook在Twitter认为违反了该平台规则的同一份声明中添加了自己的事实核查通知。现在，特朗普Facebook帖子底部的一个标签与总统的建议相矛盾，总统建议美国人尝试投票两次，以确保“邮件输入系统正常工作”。
事实核查标签上写着“邮寄投票在美国有着悠久的可信度历史，预计今年也会有同样的可信度”，这比该平台贴在其他选举相关内容上的通用投票信息标签更具体。
总统的评论是他对美国将在11月选举中依赖的邮寄投票系统的最新尝试。近几个月来，特朗普发表了许多毫无根据或彻头彻尾的虚假言论，批评邮寄投票的安全性，这是美国已经依赖的一种缺席投票制度。随着11月的临近，这些主张引起了投票权组织的关注。
VoteAmerica创始人黛布拉·克利弗(Debra Cleaver)表示：“虽然这是朝着正确方向迈出的一步，但事实仍然是，Facebook拒绝在唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的问题上执行自己的使用条款。”
“昨天，特朗普直截了当地敦促北卡罗来纳州的选民进行选民欺诈。这是特朗普利用社交媒体和其他平台散布虚假信息的更大和危险模式的一部分，目标似乎是破坏人们对美国选举的信心。“。
虽然新冠肺炎危机意味着将有比以往更多的美国人使用邮寄投票来投票，但专家们普遍认为这种投票方式是安全可靠的。
针对特朗普的言论，北卡罗来纳州选举委员会发表声明，澄清两次投票在该州属于一级重罪。
北卡罗来纳州选举委员会执行董事凯伦·布林森·贝尔(Karen Brinson Bell)表示：“在一次选举中投票两次是违法的。”
“…。试图在一次选举中投两次票或怂恿某人这样做也违反了北卡罗来纳州的法律。“
note_en:
Trump made the  initial comments in a local television interview Wednesday. “They will vote and then they are going to have to check their vote by going to the poll and voting that way because if it tabulates then they won’t be able to do that,” Trump said.
 “So let them send it in, and let them go vote. And if the system is as good as they say it is, then they obviously won’t be able to vote.”
   Twitter added a “public interest notice” to  two tweets related to those comments Thursday, citing its rules around civic and election integrity. The tweets violated the rules “specifically for encouraging people to engage in a behavior that could undermine the integrity of their individual vote,” according to Twitter spokesperson Nick Pacilio. Twitter has limited the reach of those tweets and restricted its likes, replies and retweets without comment.
 Trump’s latest attack on vote-by-mail also crossed a line for  Facebook  . The company will remove any video of Trump’s recent voting comments that are shared without context or those that support the president’s statements, though it has yet to identify any so far.
 “This video violates our policies prohibiting voter fraud and we will remove it unless it is shared to correct the record,” Facebook Policy Communications Director Andy Stone said.
 Facebook added its own fact-checking notice to the same statement that Twitter deemed in violation of that platform’s rules. Now, a label at the bottom of Trump’s Facebook post contradicts the president’s suggestion that Americans try to vote twice to make sure “the mail in system worked properly.”
  The fact-checking label, which reads “Voting by mail has a long history of trustworthiness in the US and the same is predicted this year,” is more specific than the  generic voting info label the platform attaches to other election-related content.
 The president’s comments were his latest attempt to cast doubt on the vote-by-mail systems that the U.S. will rely on in November’s election. In recent months, Trump has made many unfounded or outright false claims criticizing the safety of mail-in voting, a system that the U.S. already relies on for absentee voting. As November gets closer, those claims have voting rights organizations concerned.
 “While this is a step in the right direction, the fact remains that Facebook refuses to enforce its own Terms of Use where Donald Trump is concerned,”  VoteAmerica founder Debra Cleaver said.
 “Yesterday, Trump outright urged voters in North Carolina to commit voter fraud. This is part of a larger and dangerous pattern of Trump using social media and other platforms to distribute disinformation, with what appears to be the goal of undermining faith in US elections.”
 While the COVID-19 crisis means that more Americans than ever will be using mail-in voting to cast a ballot, the voting method is widely regarded as safe and reliable by experts.
 In response to Trump’s remarks, North Carolina’s Board of Elections issued a statement clarifying that voting twice is a Class I felony in the state.
 “It is illegal to vote twice in an election,” said Karen Brinson Bell, executive director of the North Carolina State Board of Elections.
 “… Attempting to vote twice in an election or soliciting someone to do so also is a violation of North Carolina law.”
title: 推特和脸书与特朗普角力，告诉美国人两次投票
title_en: Twitter and Facebook wrestle with Trump telling Americans to vote twice

note:
新的流程很简单，尽管一开始仍然需要一名客服人员，他们将在接近新的登机/行李投递单位之前检查旅客的身份证。如果乘客选择参加生物识别程序-这仍然是可选的-他们扫描自己的身份证，系统会将照片与机器捕获的面部扫描进行比较。
随着时间的推移，精神号希望取消让代理商检查ID的第一步，但它正在等待TSA的批准。
如果一切按计划进行，乘客就可以放下行李，愉快地前往(直到他们到达TSA检查站，但这不是航空公司的错)。
精神酒店总裁兼首席执行官泰德·克里斯蒂在今天的声明中解释说：“我们在2019年开始寻找改善入住体验的方法，作为我们投资The Guest的承诺的一部分。”“我们很早就知道，自动化和生物特征照片匹配将使办理登机手续的过程更加顺畅。现在到了2020年，我们意识到，在帮助人们感到舒适的飞行方面，同样的要素也同样有价值。限制接触点和不必要的面对面互动将改变机场的运营方式。“。
在疫情大流行之前，这看起来像是通过减少办理登机手续柜台所需的员工数量来节省资金的明显努力(自助投放行李已经在某种程度上成为一种标准程序)。现在，尽管旅行者数量保持在创纪录的低位，但感觉这一举措恰到好处。
目前，每天有600名乘客使用精神号在奥黑尔投放的行李。在测试中，航空公司发现新流程将平均处理时间缩短了70秒。
勇气号强调，所有数据都不会传输给政府，也不会离开勇气号的所有权。生物识别，特别是面部识别，长期以来一直在机场引发争议，至少在美国是这样，例如，国土安全部(Homeland Security)在登上国际航班之前测试生物识别扫描，运输安全管理局(TSA)现在测试自助检查站，让乘客通过安检线。虽然许多乘客现在只需指纹或面部扫描就可以使用Clear通过安检，但仍有很大一部分飞行公众会对此感到有点不舒服，即使在大流行期间，尽管航空公司辩称不与政府共享数据。
note_en:
The new process is straightforward, though it still involves one customer service agent at the beginning, who will check the flier’s ID before approaching the new check-in/bag-drop units. If passengers opt in to the biometric procedure — and this remains optional — they scan their ID and the system will compare the photo with a facial scan captured by the machine.
 Over time, Spirit hopes to do away with the first step of having an agent check the ID, but it is waiting for TSA approval to do so.
  If everything works according to plan, the passenger can then drop off their bags and go their merry way (until they hit the TSA checkpoint, but that’s not the airline’s fault).
  “We started looking at ways to improve the check-in experience in 2019 as part of our pledge to invest in the Guest,” Spirit President and CEO Ted Christie explained in today’s announcement. “We knew early on that automation and biometric photo-matching would make the check-in process smoother. Now in 2020, we’re realizing those same elements are just as valuable when it comes to helping people feel comfortable flying. Limiting touchpoints and unnecessary face-to-face interactions will change the way airports operate.”
 Before the pandemic, this would have looked like an obvious effort to save money by reducing the number of employees needed to run the check-in counters (with self-service bag drops having already become somewhat of a standard procedure). Now, it feels like just the right move, even as the number of travelers remains at record lows.
  Currently, 600 passengers use Spirit’s bag drop at O’Hare per day. In its tests, the airline found that the new process drops the average processing time by 70 seconds.
 Spirit stresses that none of the data is transmitted to the government and that it doesn’t leave Spirit’s possession. Biometrics, and especially facial recognition, have long been good for controversy at airports, at least in the U.S., with Homeland Security testing biometric scans before boarding international flights, for example, and the TSA now testing self-service checkpoints to get passengers through its security lines. And while a lot of fliers now feel comfortable using CLEAR to get through security with only their fingerprints or a facial scan, there is still a large chunk of the flying public that will feel somewhat uncomfortable with this, even during a pandemic and despite the airline’s argument that it doesn’t share data with the government.
title: 精神航空公司开始测试生物特征登机
title_en: Spirit Airlines starts testing biometric check-ins

note:
如果你说“游戏机”…。准确的猜测，但不完全正确。在任天堂发布这款标志性的灰兽之前的近十年里，任天堂一直在制造Game&Amp；Watch，这是一款手持设备的集合，每台设备只致力于玩一两个简单的游戏，偶尔还可以兼作时钟使用。
任天堂(Nintendo)今天上午宣布，游戏与安卓手表(Game&Amp；Watch)将推出现代化的限量版回归，其怀旧之情的敲击方式几乎没有其他公司能做到。
作为超级马里奥兄弟35周年庆典的一部分，这部电影完全以马里奥为主题，并恰如其分地取名为“游戏与游戏观察：超级马里奥兄弟”(Game&Am；Watch：Super Mario Bros.)。
与最初的Game&Amp；Watch阵容一样，这个阵容似乎意味着它可以玩的不同游戏的数量相当有限。在官方产品页面上，任天堂提到了“超级马里奥兄弟”(Super Mario Bros.)、“超级马里奥兄弟2：迷失的等级”(Super Mario Bros.2：The Lost Level)(或者就是日本人所说的“超级马里奥兄弟2”)，以及马里奥皮肤翻拍的“球”(Ball)，这是1980年4月推出的第一款游戏和安培手表(Game&Amp；Watch)。所以在所有的…中有三场比赛。但考虑到我们在以前的设备上所看到的情况，如果粉丝们打开它，让它在没有时间的情况下运行更多，我也不会感到惊讶。
40(！)。因此，任天堂在这款游戏与安卓手表中偷偷地进行了一些升级，这些升级在今天可能看起来像是赠品。例如，它有一个全彩色液晶显示屏，而最初的显示屏是黑白的-而且你可以通过USB-C给它充电，而不是必须烧毁一堆纽扣电池。任天堂表示，每次充电应该能持续8个小时左右。
当你没有玩其中的一款游戏时，这个东西就会变成一个便携的小钟(因此它的名字中有“&amp；Watch”的一部分)，总共有35个不同的马里奥主题场景。如果任天堂的时钟功能做对了，我可以看到这些东西在很多人的办公桌上赢得了永久的位置。
尽管任天堂指出，这将是一次“有限的”运行，但他们并没有具体说明他们将生产多少台…。虽然预购细节“很快就会到来”，但他们不会比这更具体。他们确实说它将于11月13日发货，建议零售价为50美元…。但除此之外，如果你担心拿到这些东西中的一个，你会想要密切关注更多的细节。
note_en:
If you said “Game Boy”… solid guess, but not quite. For nearly a decade before Nintendo released that iconic gray beast, it was making the Game & Watch — a collection of handheld devices, each dedicated to playing just one or two simple games and, occasionally, doubling as a clock.
 Hammering that nostalgia button in a way that few other companies can, Nintendo announced this morning that the Game & Watch will be making a modernized, but limited edition, return.
  Released as part of the celebration around the 35th anniversary of Super Mario Bros., it’s fully Mario themed — and, appropriately, called “Game & Watch: Super Mario Bros.”
 As with the original Game & Watch lineup, it seems like this one is meant to be pretty limited in the number of different titles it can play. On the  official product page, Nintendo mentions “Super Mario Bros.,” “Super Mario Bros. 2: The Lost Levels” (or just “Super Mario Bros. 2,” as it was known in Japan) and a Mario-skinned remake of “Ball,” the first Game & Watch title that shipped back in April of 1980. So three games in all… but given what we’ve seen happen with previous devices like this, I wouldn’t be surprised if the fans crack it open and have it running a whole lot more than that in no time flat.
  A lot has changed in 40(!) years, so Nintendo is sneaking a few upgrades into this Game & Watch that probably seem like givens today. It has a full-color LCD, for example, whereas the original displays were black and white — and you’ll be able to charge it over USB-C, rather than having to burn through a stack of button cell batteries. Nintendo says it should last around eight hours per charge.
  When you’re not playing one of the included games, this thing turns into a little portable clock (thus the “& Watch” part of its name), with 35 different Mario-themed scenes in all. If Nintendo does that clock feature right, I can see these things earning a permanent spot on a lot of people’s desks.
 While Nintendo notes that it’ll be a “limited” run, they haven’t said exactly how many of these they’ll be making… and while pre-order details are “coming soon,” they’re not getting more specific than that. They  do say it’ll ship on November 13th with an MSRP of $50… but beyond that, if you’re worried about getting one of these, you’ll want to keep an eye out for more details.
title: 任天堂正在重新制造其制造的第一款便携式游戏系统
title_en: Nintendo is remaking the first portable gaming system it ever built
